text.title: Automation Candidate Assessment
text.summary: Six-criterion scoring matrix for automation readiness, RPA vs. intelligent automation comparison, and key vendor landscape.

rule.statement: Score every automation candidate against six criteria: Volume (high = more than 100 transactions/day), Standardization (high = rule-based with few exceptions), Stability (medium = process unchanged more than 6 months), Digital Input (medium = structured data available), Error Rate (medium = high manual error frequency), ROI Potential (high = high labor cost with measurable payback). Total out of 30. Recommend: full automation above 24, partial automation 18-24, process improvement first 12-18, not suitable below 12.
rule.rationale: Without a scoring matrix, automation selection is based on who has the loudest stakeholder voice. The matrix forces objective measurement across dimensions that predict automation success.

list.item: RPA: rule-based repetitive tasks, predefined decision logic, structured data only, no learning, human escalation for exceptions — best for high-volume stable processes

Intelligent Automation: complex variable tasks, AI-driven decisions, structured and unstructured data, continuous learning, autonomous exception handling — best for variable processes with document complexity or judgment requirements
UiPath: largest pure-play RPA, enterprise leader, strong developer ecosystem and test automation integration
Automation Anywhere: cloud-native architecture, strong AI integration, IQ Bot for document processing
Blue Prism: enterprise governance and auditability focus, preferred in regulated industries
Microsoft Power Automate: M365 integration, low barrier for Microsoft shops, desktop and cloud flows
Celonis: process mining combined with automation recommendations, action engine triggers RPA from discovered bottlenecks

section.title: Market Context
section.p: RPA market: $35.27 billion in 2026 growing to $247.34 billion by 2035. 58% of enterprises will use RPA with AI/ML by 2026. 44% already use AI-enabled RPA for document processing. By 2026, 30% of enterprises will automate more than 50% of network activities.
